title: I Visited America's Most "Christian" College
body: I'm from the town LU is in. They actually own a decent chunk of the town, having bought shopping centers, the mall, and much more. Despite what it sounds like, they have actually heavily invested in them, not only revitalizing them, but renovating them as well. The mall was honestly a run down ghost town a couple years ago, but now it is a place where people actually go to be with their friends which is something that was unheard of before.

title: I Visited America's Most "Christian" College
body:
As a 57 year old truck driver who travels around America with my little puppy Trucker ! It gives me so much hope in the new generation. To see kids like you doing what you're doing, it's awesome. My 2 go to pastors, John MacArthur and Voddie Baucham has recently gone to be with the Lord.
An event so sad that they're gone. And I don't have to wonder who's gonna pick up the torch. It's good to see people like you. So go into all the world and preach the gospel to every creature.
Godspeed!!!

title: I Visited America's Most "Christian" College
body:
Just graduated from Liberty last Spring. I think the circumstance that demonstrated the kindness among the students the most was when I had to spend a week in the hospital due to an unforeseen medical emergency. I had a wealth of friends caring for me in that time. People were staying with me until the latest hours of the night. My RA organized a rotating group of friends to come visit me. I got so many cards and messages. People were continuously praying for me. When I got discharged and got to come back, I was welcomed with countless smiles and cheers. In the months that followed, so many people showed selflessness in helping me along in my recovery. In honesty, I’m tearing up again just thinking about it. Liberty may have gotten an unfortunate reputation due to some things in its past, and it’s certainly not perfect. But I don’t know if I ever have it ever will find a place this side of Heaven where there is such a large concentration of people who love the Lord and one another so strongly. The Lord has blessed that college :)
(And the academics are good too, not Ivy League or anything, but don’t let people guilt you into thinking everyone there is dumb)

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body: I respectfully disagree with your opinion. No way Rutgers (#40) and UMD (#46) are tier 4 IMO. Admissions rate do not equate to quality or outcomes - and public universities are required to be accessible for their in-state residents instead of exclusive.

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body: Acceptance rate doesn't mean a school is more prestigious.  This just means they get way more applicants for the vacant spots.  If a school with 1spot got 1,000 applications their acceptance rate would be 0.001%.  If that same school received 1 applicant their rate would be 100% acceptance.

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body:
You have to base it on majors and departments

Like, Rutgers, for example, has an amazing CS and engineering department, Liberty has a great game development program

What's a tier 1 is gonna be different for me as it would for others.

Some schools surprisingly get harder to get into, the higher the reputation a department gets

Or hell, the stock of a college could rise as you get into said college (happened with me doing IT game dev at NJIT from 2012-2016, program was new but it was relatively on par with something you'd see at other game PROGRAMMING CS programs, and it blended in super well for me when I went into software engineering when I graduated)

Using NJIT as an example, you could easily get in for history or theater

But hands down would it be MUCH harder getting into their biomedical engineering program. It depends on the majors, lol

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body: Just FYI, these tiers are (generally) irrelevant if you stay and work in the US. Sure, maybe if you are from a foreign country and go back there after college and tell your neighbor "I graduated from Harvard" they'll be incredibly impressed -- but if you tell them "I graduate from Caltech" or Stanford they might not even know those universities. The reality is, Stanford is much better than Harvard in some fields while the opposite is also true. I chose UCSD for my undergrad in Neuroscience over UPenn and Cornell and it wasn't even a difficult choice -- simply speaking, the research and depth of study there was a better fit and dare I say even more prestigious. No one on the west coast, by the way, will ever undermine your academic ability if you graduated from Cal or UCLA instead of Brown or Princeton. If you are admitted to any school within the first "3 tiers" you'll have similar if not identical quality of education. Yes, going to a private university does have some advantages to consider over going to a public university but these don't often justify higher costs. It is much better to go to UCI, for example, with their Reagent's Scholarship than to Yale and graduating with $100,000+ in student debt. You can always do incredibly well in a public university and then do your PhD at a private university for "free" (PhD's are often fully funded with research stipends or TA positions)
author: @eric1172

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body: UMD is not a tier 4 school, its acceptance rate is 40% and rutgers & temple both have acceptance rates 20-30% higher than UMD yet are in the same category. It should also be noted that a public 40% accpt rate is more comp than a lot of privates aritifically inflated lower accpt rate and that can be measured by the GPA, SAT score, and income outcomes out of college which are all measured by usnews. UMD is a t45 institution and a flagship state worth its salt.
author: @aldrianspalding4157

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body: This is unfortunately inaccurate since your video mixes some schools which are basically top 50-60 schools into Tier 4. Suggested improvement: list range of grades, scores, acceptance rates and geography distribution (often this says how respected the school is outside its region). Also you mistake the gap between 1,2 and 3 v 4 and 5. Your gap is actually 1-2, 3-4 (you seem to place top 60 schools into Tier 4)  and then 5. But then Tier 5 seems quite large or defies description (there is a very large range from decent private or state schools to 2-year colleges). So then you are also missing several other tiers. Also your description of "to go to college to have fun and make their family proud" and "trying a lot harder than students in Tier 4 and 5" have NO EVIDENCE. I would say those statements are unusual generalizations.
author: @10sDog

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body: I got into Columbia!!! I am sooo happy. It was the first and the only university I applied to. Not kidding the only university I applied for, and I got in lol!!!
author: @reactionscrolls

title: College Rankings: 5 Tiers of Colleges in the United States
body:
Idk about other disciplines but UMD (U Maryland - College Park) is extremely selective in STEM, and it’s definitely tier 1. I’m going to UMD but also got accepted to UChicago and Northwestern for PhD EE/CS with very high honor awards at those schools (full tuition + $5xxxx/yr assistantship). I’m not sure about the general public, but to the world-class experts in many STEM fields, UMD is a big name, especially in CS/Physics/Math. When I presented and did the networking at several premier conferences in both CS and physical sciences (ICML, AMS, AGU), it’s super easy to break the ice by saying I’m an UMD student since those top people often know/has worked with several other top scientists from UMD.

The same can be said for Berkeley, just that Berkeley is on their own league academically along with MIT/Stanford/Harvard. Berkeley is a huge name with extreme prestige and is mad respected by the most intelligent people on the planet but often overlooked by average folks.
author: @AndrewN-xu6cr

title: Liberty University Online Review | Best Online Colleges for Busy Adults
body: I'm an LUO student, and I love it. I work better in an online environment and will be graduating with my bachelor's in August. I have had a really great experience. :)
author: @jeannine8400

title: Liberty University Online Review | Best Online Colleges for Busy Adults
body: After a horrific experience at a brick-and-mortar master's program that relied on its historic and elitist reputation, instead of actually teaching its students, I went to Liberty and got my first master's online and now I'm working on my MBA. Absolutely had no problems getting the classes I wanted and the professors are awesome. At the end of the day, no one cares where you got your degree, as long as it's accredited and you have the experience to back it up. No way will I ever step foot on a college campus ever again and Liberty University has helped me achieve my goals with affordable master's programs that are military-friendly.
author: @drew8979

title: Liberty University Online Review | Best Online Colleges for Busy Adults

author: @wardout5695

title: Liberty University Online Review | Best Online Colleges for Busy Adults
body:
Your videos are very helpful.

When I attended Liberty U Online, I found it to be good. However, one particular year attending it seemed they outsourced professors from other states and these professors just didn't have the professionalism, organization, quick response, flexibility, training to be a Liberty professor, positive/encouragement vibe, nor Christian values as professors located actually at Liberty. So that was a disappointment. I just made sure next semester I chose professors physically located at Liberty. I enjoyed the online professors much more that were located on campus. They were great and yes seem to be at a higher standard. This was years ago, so maybe it has changed.

Tip: Just try to schedule online courses with professors at the actually campus if you can. A better college experience in my opinion.
author: @EagleArrow

title: Liberty University Online Review | Best Online Colleges for Busy Adults
body: I am currently attending SNHU and am considering transferring for a number of reasons. First being they are a very liberal college with very liberal curriculum and material including instructors. I am considering LUO because I am conservative, I am also a Christian. So for I have completed a little over one year of my BS and got to the point I literally hated it. I am a Veteran and really would like to finish my degree and have another avenue to provide for my family.

title: Day in the Life of a Harvard Law Student | EXTENDED Week in the Life Version
body:
"Most law-school classrooms are arranged in roughly the same way. Broken usually by two aisles, concentric semicircles of seats and desks issue back from the podium, resulting in a kind of amphitheater. In Pound, where we had met Mann, the newly constructed classrooms had been built with remarkable compactness. But in Austin the rooms were ancient and enormous. The seats and desks were in rows of yellowed oak, tiered steeply toward the rear. At its highest, the classroom was nearly forty feet, with long, heavy curtains on the windows and dark portraits of English judges, dressed in their wigs and robes, hanging in gilt frames high on the wall. It was an awesome setting, especially when its effect was combined with the stories we had all heard about Perini. There was a tone of tense humor in the conversations around me, most voices somewhat hushed. As I headed for my seat, I overheard a number of people say, 'I don’t want it to be me,' referring to whom Perini would call on."
- Scott Turow; "One L"
author: @sce2aux464

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: As a navy vet going to Liberty, I find it very funny when people talk about having to sign out to go places. Having experienced life in the military, the restrictions at Liberty are very minimal in comparison. You want to experience strict regulations and guidelines on  your freedom to do whatever you want, join the military.
author: @justinrosa357

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: I think other college students take it to heart because they think being nasty and having bad habits is good. Liberty makes it so that you don't fall through the cracks and have a good moral stance and bei g more professional.
author: @Nram10

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body:
Tidbit of info:
The reason LU has a 99% acceptance rate is because of its online school - largest in the country and its very military friendly. If you look at just the residential data the acceptance rate is around 50%.

Thank you for making the effort to tour campus and see what it’s like versus assuming it’s some cult-driven weirdo place. If you find yourself in the area again late morning on a Wed or Fri, consider attending convocation. I get that Christianity/religion might not be your thing, but the atmosphere is so vibrant and inspiring! I’ve only attended a few times, but I leave just feeling good about life!
author: @mrskimthomas06

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: If you attend Liberty, you're a Christian, so these rules are a small price to pay in exchange for a lot of the resources that you get access to like Convocation and regular worship. It's truly a beautiful campus too and with the loneliness crisis the country has, I think young people are more willing to seek out spirituality than they were a decade ago.
author: @GbawlZ

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: As a current student at Liberty, I really appreciated this video! I love it here, but I really did enjoy hearing a perspective from outside the community that this school is catered to. I really appreciated the way you treated this with respect and I'm glad you were able to come and visit!
author: @thomasyu2047

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: Liberty is training students to be prepared for any type of professional job.  Most any profession a student will enter after college will have a code of conduct.  Most jobs will expect you to follow a dress code, keep your area clean, show up on time, let them know if you need days off, not drink alcohol on the job, conduct yourself professionally with coworkers, refrain from public display of affection in the workplace, and show up for required meetings. Subjecting yourself to the rules and expectations at Liberty University seems shocking to the general public but, in my opinion, Liberty students are learning how to conduct themselves in preparation for the professional world.
author: @lauracummings1625

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: LU Grad ‘16. Absolutely loved my time there. They gave me a lot of opportunities that I probably would not have gotten anywhere else. Met my wife there as well. Will forever be grateful for Liberty.
author: @Ethan_Brown34

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: One of my kids goes to Liberty and loves it - so do I. The rules are not overly restrictive and serve numerous purposes, all positive.  There's good reason you don't hear swearing on campus, people smile and talk to each other, hold doors for you, and work hard on their studies. Convocation is not a punishment. it's a phenomenal experience, including giving the entire student body the opportunity to see, hear, and even meet world leaders, sports stars,  actors, and religious and thought leaders from across the spectrum. I tune in to the live feed every chance I get. Everything is clean and well maintained, compared to the big name school my other child attends. The food is pretty amazing for a college cafeteria. I went to a big name college and the lack of restrictions probably had a negative impact on my experience and my education.   Liberty does things right. The academics are thorough and challenging and their degrees should be highly respected, as well as their graduates- because you know you know they have good work ethic and most likely have strong Christian morals...which are very good, not harmful, as so many media outlets might have you believe today. Go Flames!
author: @edominek

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body:
I graduated in 1987, and when I went there, it was a lot more strict than it is now. I loved all four years that I spent there, and I still love the school today.
I think you gave a pretty fair representation for a one-day tour. The school is different and it is not for everyone. If you’re mean going live is to get drunk every weekend and party Liberty is not for you. They keep you busy with a lot of activities and of course your studies. The curfew is not such a big deal. There are many exceptions to the curfew, and besides that there’s not a whole lot to do in Lynchburg after 12 o’clock at night.
author: @lancethorson6564

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: Oh man I’m glad I saw this video cause I was with you on the tour! I am actually a tennis player that was thinking of transferring to liberty and when I heard you say that, I immediately texted my trainer and told him to book a whole week of non stop training because you looked comp and I needed to step up! I can cancel that now
author: @black.caillou

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: Thank you for this review. I was a residential grad student and loved my time at LU. Its not for everyone, but anyone is welcome to attend at Liberty. I had fellow grad students with a variety of political, social, and relligious views and all were welcome. LU fostered a respectful dialogue of differeing opinions in my classes.

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: I'm in their online high school and am so excited to go to Liberty and hoping I make the D1 hockey. This video made me more excited.

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: If you come to LU again, let any of us LU students know and we can show you around to other areas :) the town was built around the college so there is a TON to do (non religious things)! Thank you for the unbiased opinion 🙏
author: @felicitysurprenant

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body:
Good, fair take on the University. Good job.
The military is similar in restrictions, but more strict. I think it’s good. I would have appreciated that from a university when I was that age, if I would have not dropped out of college. Kids under 26 still need to learn how to become responsible citizens even when most reject that idea in their 20s. In my 20s, I went wild and didn’t calm down until I met Jesus Christ at 30 years of age. If people were able to relive their 20s, they would not say they wished they had partied more. After all said and done, partying and living for the quick highs/dopamine hits was overrated and led to nowhere.
author: @rocco1267

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: My son is a freshman at LU. He loves it but not a fan of the curfew, forced to attend Comp & room checks. He does plan to move off but despite that he chose it & loves it.
author: @annescarter

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: My son starts at Liberty next week. Lol. I appreciate your honest and open opinion. He was sold immediately but I understand the questions. Enjoyed the video
author: @pamelam7696

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: As someone who goes to liberty and is graduating pretty soon, I’m actually surprised by some of the rules. I literally see couples on the third floor of Demos full-blown making out and nobody, even the professors do anything. I heard of a friend who got caught making out in the parking lot of Thomas Road Baptist Church and the officer from LUPD just asked them to leave. Didn’t take any information or anything. While the rules are enforced, I feel like the website makes it sound scarier than they actually are. Also, if you’re above 21 curfew doesn’t really apply. You just need to sign out with a special form and promise to not go crazy and start drinking or doing drugs
author: @princesse0920

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body:
Not the strictest in any way, shape, or form! Try Pensacola Christian or Hyles Anderson. Liberty is a great place to learn in a positive school environment. Kids aren’t there to get drunk, high, or have sex every night and weekend. Who wouldn’t want their kid to avoid that kind of life destruction? They do enough college-aged behaviors there without excessive debauchery.
I do wish the academics were stronger with better quality professors, but that could probably be said for many universities around the U.S.
author: @tammyvogel259

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: As someone who works for the school and went to liberty university for undergrad I love the friends and people who I have friendship with. Also work with
author: @Brick_man_dan_2000

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: I spent my first 2 years at a state school and then transferred to liberty for my last 2 years and I have to say that the difference in the quality of your education is night and day. The quality of liberty’s professors is second to none. My personal experience with liberty was incredible. I have to say, a state school has nothing on Liberty.
author: @RoyceDay45

title: I Investigated America's STRICTEST University
body: When we went to visit, the students seemed really happy. ALL the ones we just happened to run into randomly, said it grew their faith. Also, it's a D1 school, so it gets tons of money (and is expensive to attend- though Financial Aid really helps a LOT to make it affordable). The campus is gorgeous! It is a CHRISTIAN college, so complaining about values being held by a conservative Christian college, is simply silly. You wouldn't knock an Islamic school for having values that align with the Quran, so the rules absolutely make sense. Go where you believe the values will align with you, but don't go somewhere where it's obvious and already understood what the rules will be. I agree about the cramped dorm rooms. My college dorm room was way bigger, but some of the apartments on campus are pretty big.  We loved the college, the peaceful feeling you get when you step on campus. Beds made shouldn't be a thing, but the rest is to avoid having to spray the roach control chemicals multiple times a year.  Everyone we know who has gone there, loved it. As with anything, go by the majority of reviews by the actual students.
author: @lauranglover521
